old_value: {"id":"better-task-manager-wba","title":"Fix zoom-to-task using positionAbsolute from RF store","description":"When clicking a task in the all-epics view, the viewport pans to the epic group corner instead of the task. Root cause: onNodeClick uses node.position (relative to parent) instead of positionAbsolute. Fix: expose getNode from PanController via ref and use it to read the live node with positionAbsolute.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"skr@whiteawaygroup.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T21:21:41Z","created_by":"Rasmus Skriver","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:21:41Z"}

new_value: Fixed: PanController now exposes getNode via ref; onNodeClick uses getNode(node.id) to read positionAbsolute from the RF store instead of relying on the potentially-relative node.position from the click callback argument.
buildGraph.ts split complete: graphConstants.ts + graphStructure.ts + elkLayout.ts, thin orchestrator with re-exports, all quality gates pass
buildGraph.ts split complete: graphConstants.ts + graphStructure.ts + elkLayout.ts, thin orchestrator with re-exports, all quality gates pass
buildGraph.ts split complete: graphConstants.ts + graphStructure.ts + elkLayout.ts, thin orchestrator with re-exports, all quality gates pass

Second fix: React Flow v12 moved positionAbsolute to node.internals.positionAbsolute. Changed PanController to expose getInternalNode (not getNode), and onNodeClick now reads absPos from internalNode.internals.positionAbsolute.
